task_title: Wire the OpenTelemetry SDK into API + worker
diff_excerpt: 4 files: services/api/treadmill_api/observability.py (+22 -16), services/api/tests/test_observability.py (+107 new), workers/agent/treadmill_agent/observability.py (+22 -16), workers/agent/tests/test_observability.py (+101 new). Plus the same PR's later commit (907b9c2) wired configure() into services/api/treadmill_api/cli.py:33,139 and workers/agent/treadmill_agent/__main__.py:22,31; added treadmill.api.startup span in app.py:210-211 and treadmill.worker.step span in runner.py:249-250. See PR #97 for full diff.
task_spec_excerpt: Add the OpenTelemetry SDK + OTLP exporter + auto-instrumentations as runtime deps. Create observability.py modules with TracerProvider + MeterProvider + (logger handler). Call configure() from services/api/treadmill_api/cli.py and workers/agent/__main__.py. Add custom spans: treadmill.api.startup and treadmill.worker.step. (Full spec: docs/plans/2026-05-12-observability-stack.md task otel-sdk-foundation).

judge_output_actual.rationale: The required DIAGRAM_SOURCE was not provided, so conformance cannot be verified. A compliance gate must not pass on missing evidence. Additionally, comparing the diff against the task-spec prose (the only available reference) reveals several gaps: (1) _logger_handler is removed from both modules while the spec requires 'TracerProvider + MeterProvider + (logger handler)'; (2) the spec requires wiring configure() into services/api/treadmill_api/cli.py and workers/agent/__main__.py, but neither file appears in the diff; (3) the required treadmill.api.startup and treadmill.worker.step custom spans are absent from the diff.

label: pass
label_rationale: Architect on sonnet verified by file:line that every spec item the judge claimed missing was actually present in the branch's commit history (cli.py:33,139 imports + calls configure_observability; __main__.py:22,31 same; app.py:210-211 startup span; runner.py:249-250 worker step span). The judge fired defensively because no DIAGRAM_SOURCE was provided and fell back to task-spec-prose comparison with 'must not pass on missing evidence' bias — a known failure mode pre-empted by PR #109's surgical prompt fix and to be generalized by ADR-0040/ADR-0041.
commentary: This is the canonical no-diagram-cited false positive that motivated #109's prompt fix. After #109, the judge should return pass with rationale 'no diagram cited; rule has nothing to enforce' on this same input. Train DSPy to reinforce that behavior.
